config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama karena tanpa perlindungan yang kuat, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas dan membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Di Indonesia, kita sering melihat alasan demi ketertiban atau kepentingan umum dipakai terlalu longgar, padahal justru hak warga yang lemah perlu dijaga paling keras. Kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh menginjak hak dasar orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States that logic has often been used to excuse major abuses against minorities and dissenters. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every case; public health, safety, and fair laws do justify some limits. The real rule should be that society can restrict individual freedom only when it is necessary, narrowly tailored, and accountable, not just because officials claim it is for the common good. If we flip the priority too far toward society, we risk turning rights into privileges that can be taken away whenever they become inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi umum” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membungkam kritik dan mengorbankan kelompok kecil. Yang lebih masuk akal adalah hak individu jadi dasar, lalu dibatasi hanya kalau benar-benar perlu, jelas alasannya, dan diawasi ketat agar tidak jadi alat penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan publik memang penting, terutama soal keselamatan dan ketertiban, tetapi kalau hak dasar bisa dikalahkan begitu saja, yang paling sering rugi justru warga biasa. Jadi, bagi saya, masyarakat kuat bukan karena individu dikorbankan, melainkan karena hak tiap orang dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because in the United States we have repeatedly seen “society first” language used to justify oppression, censorship, and discrimination. That said, I also do not believe individual rights are a blank check; public safety, health, and a functioning legal system can justify limited restrictions when they are truly necessary and tightly controlled. What matters is not giving society blanket priority, but proving that any limit on a person’s rights is narrow, accountable, and the least harmful option. If we make “the interests of society” the top principle by default, rights become fragile and easy to strip away whenever the majority gets impatient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai sebagai alasan umum untuk membatasi kritik, menggusur hak kelompok kecil, atau menutup ruang bagi yang tidak berkuasa. Menurut saya, hak individu harus jadi titik awal, lalu boleh dibatasi hanya jika benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu lebih nyaman. Kalau kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas mutlak, yang sering terjadi justru bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan warga biasa yang makin mudah dikorbankan. Kepentingan publik penting, tetapi legitimasi negara justru terlihat dari kemampuannya melindungi hak orang yang paling rentan, bukan hanya memenuhi keinginan mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that kind of thinking has too often been used to justify abuse by majorities and governments. That said, I do not treat individual rights as unlimited; public safety, health, and basic order can justify narrow restrictions when they are clearly necessary and accountable. The problem with putting society first is that it sounds noble but can easily become a blank check to silence dissent or sacrifice minorities. A free society is strongest when it protects individual rights by default and only limits them with strict justification.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
